19.reason: [The loop condition 'i<=p' causes the loop to run only once, but it should shift elements from p to q-1 to restore the array. It should be 'i < q' to properly reverse the effect of swap.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The loop condition 'i<=p' causes the loop to run only once, but it should shift elements from p to q-1 to restore the array. It should be 'i < q' to properly reverse the effect of swap.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The loop condition 'i<=p' causes the loop to run only once, but it should shift elements from p to q-1 to restore the original array. It should be 'i < q' to properly reverse the effect of swap.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [The loop condition 'i<=p' causes the loop to run only once, and when i==p, accessing num[i+1] (i.e., num[p+1]) may be incorrect. More critically, this loop does not properly reverse the effect of 'swap'. It should iterate from p to q-1 to shift elements left, but currently it only runs one iteration regardless of q. This leads to incorrect array state during backtracking, causing wrong permutations or undefined behavior.]
